Downtown Honolulu Business Improvement District (expanded from Fort Street Mall SID No. 2; Ord. 25-47 / Bill 51) [City and County of Honolulu / Downtown] https://hnldoc.ehawaii.gov/hnldoc/measure/3401; Waikīkī Beach Special Improvement District Association (SID No. 3) [City and County of Honolulu / Waikīkī Beach] https://www.wbsida.org/overview; Kailua Village Business Improvement District (No. 1) [County of Hawaiʻi / Kailua-Kona] https://historickailuavillage.com/; Kailua Village BID Ordinance and Boundary Map [County of Hawaiʻi / Kailua-Kona] https://historickailuavillage.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/KVBID-Ordinance.pdf; Kauai County South Kauai Community Plan (notes no BID authorization yet under HRS 46-80.5) [County of Kauaʻi] https://www.kauai.gov/files/assets/public/planning-department/documents/skcp_chapter6.pdf
next_action: Exhaustively inventory all active SIDs/BIDs via county clerk ordinance lists, Appendix 28-A updates, recent bills, and finance/tax assessment records for Honolulu (confirm only Nos. 1-3), Hawaiʻi (confirm only Kailua Village No. 1; Hilo rejected), Maui (search for any established under Ch. 3.70), and Kauai (confirm none); extract or request parcel-level boundaries/maps/GIS from district associations, county real property tax offices, or open data portals (none found statewide or countywide); recheck DCCA business registry only for management nonprofits and DBEDT/AG for any overlooked registry.
notes: Statewide enabling is HRS §46-80.5 (Special improvement district), allowing charter counties to create SIDs by ordinance for supplemental maintenance/security, environmental/natural resource/hazard/climate work, and other services/improvements that restore/promote business activity, via special assessments (flexible methodology), optional bonds, and nonprofit association management (Ch. 414D). These are the legal BID equivalents. Honolulu implements via ROH Ch. 28 (SIDs Nos. 1 Waikiki ~2000, 2 Fort Street Mall expanded 2025 via Bill 51/Ord. 25-47 to Downtown Honolulu BID, 3 Waikiki Beach 2015); Hawaiʻi County via Code Ch. 35 (explicitly BIDs; active Kailua Village No. 1; Downtown Hilo No. 2 proposed but rejected 2023); Maui via Ord. 4382 adding Ch. 3.70 (enabling only; no active districts confirmed); Kauai has not authorized per community plan. Distinguish from traditional improvement districts (HRS 46-80), community facilities districts (HRS 46-80.1 / ROH Ch. 27), tax increment, zoning/design special districts (e.g., Honolulu zoning special districts GIS—different purpose/use), and HCDA districts. No statewide registry, comprehensive official list, or unified GIS of BID/SID boundaries (statewide geoportal and county open data lack them; district sites provide some maps/descriptions). Coverage partial: authority + all county enabling codes + known active districts (Honolulu-centric + one Hawaiʻi) verified with high confidence; full active inventory, status of any Maui districts, and digital boundaries incomplete. Sparse/failed searches (e.g., Kauai active, statewide maps) do not prove absence.

local_terms: Business Improvement District; BID; Local Business Improvement District
known_local_sources: Downtown Boise Business Improvement District / Downtown Boise Association [Boise] https://downtownboise.org/about/business-improvement-district; Nampa Business Improvement District [Nampa] https://www.cityofnampa.us/1253/Business-Improvement-District; Downtown Caldwell Business Improvement District / Destination Caldwell [Caldwell] https://www.cityofcaldwell.org/Business/Business-Improvement-District-Information; Downtown Idaho Falls Business Improvement District [Idaho Falls] https://downtownidahofalls.com/about-us/business-improvement-district/; Coeur d'Alene Downtown Business Improvement District / Downtown Association [Coeur d'Alene] https://cdadowntown.com/about/; Downtown Lewiston Business Improvement District [Lewiston] https://www.cityoflewiston.org/661/Business-Improvement-District; Historic Downtown Pocatello Business Improvement District [Pocatello] https://www.historicdowntownpocatello.com/explore-downtown; Idaho Statutes Title 50 Chapter 26 Business Improvement Districts (full chapter) [Statewide] https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title50/T50CH26/; Idaho Code 50-1703A Local Business Improvement Districts [Statewide] https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title50/T50CH17/SECT50-1703A/
next_action: Expand inventory via additional city municipal codes/ordinances, association directories (e.g., International Downtown Association, Association of Idaho Cities), and municipal GIS/open data portals for remaining cities (e.g., Twin Falls, Meridian status, Sandpoint current status); seek any boundary GeoJSON/shapefiles.
notes: Enabling authority verified as Idaho Code Title 50 Chapter 26 (Business Improvement Districts, authorized 1980, purposes include parking, public space improvements, events, transportation, retail promotion; special assessments on businesses/business property; cities may contract for administration). Distinct from broader Local Improvement Districts (LIDs, Title 50 Chapter 17 for capital infrastructure like streets/utilities, financed by assessments/bonds). Separate limited provision at 50-1703A (Local Business Improvement Districts, 1987) for exterior building improvements to match city architectural themes (requires 100% parcel owner consent, assessed as LIDs, max 20% of market value). No statewide registry, inventory, or central GIS of BIDs located after statute, government, and open-data searches (failed search ≠ none exist). Known active BIDs per official/district sources in Boise (est. ~1987), Nampa (~1985), Caldwell, Idaho Falls (~1990s, renewal 2027), Coeur d'Alene (1990), Lewiston (2020), Pocatello (1991); Sandpoint had one historically (dissolution attempt ~2010 failed; current status unclear/possibly inactive per some planning docs). Meridian has discussed/proposed but documents note operating without one. DBA notes 'at least six' in Idaho (likely undercount). Boundaries available as maps/PDFs on several district/city pages but no statewide or easily downloadable GeoJSON/ArcGIS layers confirmed. Distinctions from urban renewal/URDs and other special districts observed. Candidate sources are research leads only.

local_terms: Business Improvement District; BID; Special Service Area; SSA
known_local_sources: Business Improvement Districts (BID) and Special Service Areas (SSA) Program + Provider List [City of Chicago] https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/dcd/supp_info/special_service_areassaprogram.html; Special Service Area (SSA) Provider List with maps [City of Chicago] https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/dcd/supp_info/special_service_areasandproviderlist.html; Boundaries - Special Service Areas (GIS/open data) [City of Chicago] https://data.cityofchicago.org/Community-Economic-Development/Boundaries-Special-Service-Areas/cmr6-dn8c; SSA map / related SSA datasets [City of Chicago] https://data.cityofchicago.org/Community-Economic-Development/SSA-map/2k7v-9xvk; Special Service Areas [City of Peoria] https://www.peoriagov.org/1023/Special-Service-Areas; Special Service Area Districts (GIS) [Peoria County] https://data-peoriacountygis.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/special-service-area-districts/explore; Special Service Area Tax District - Historical (GIS open data) [Cook County] https://hub-cookcountyil.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/special-service-area-tax-district-historical-2023/about; Business Improvement Districts (BID) and Special Service Areas (SSA) Program [City of Chicago] https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/dcd/supp_info/special_service_areassaprogram.html; Boundaries - Special Service Areas (GIS/open data) [City of Chicago] https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/kjav-iyuj; Special Service Areas and Tax Increment Finance Areas (GIS) [Lake County] https://data-lakecountyil.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/special-service-areas-and-tax-increment-finance-areas/about; Special Service Areas (Taxing Units) GIS layer [Kendall County] https://maps.co.kendall.il.us/portal/home/item.html?id=7906b769181f412db9960cce7e82e9ef; Special Service Area 5 (Bass Street Landing) [City of Moline] https://www.moline.il.us/1419/Special-Service-Area-5
next_action: Continue systematic review of additional large/medium municipalities and remaining counties (e.g., DuPage, Kane, Will, Winnebago/Rockford, Sangamon/Springfield, McLean) via city/county GIS portals, clerk tax extension reports, assessor maps, and ordinance searches for SSAs; monitor Chicago BID designations post-2026 applications; check IDOR or Comptroller for any non-public inventory references that could become public; distinguish pure residential/stormwater SSAs if needed but retain BID-equivalent business corridor uses.
notes: Illinois authorizes classic BIDs under Business Improvement District Law (65 ILCS 125/, P.A. 103-646, eff. 7-1-24), limited to municipalities >500,000 population (Chicago only). Chicago DPD accepts feasibility applications for BIDs (deadline Nov 1, 2026 for 2027 start); none active as of research (law recent; BIDs cannot overlap SSAs). Longstanding statewide equivalent is Special Service Areas under Special Service Area Tax Law (35 ILCS 200/27-5 et seq. / Article 27 of Property Tax Code), available to municipalities and counties for special services/improvements (including downtown/business corridor marketing, security, cleaning, beautification) funded by property tax levies solely on the SSA. Creation via local ordinance after notice, hearing, petition/objection process; maps/ordinances filed with county clerks/recorders. Chicago has ~58 active SSAs with authoritative provider lists, citywide maps, and downloadable GIS boundaries. Examples confirmed elsewhere (Peoria, Lake/Cook/Peoria/Kendall Counties GIS, Moline, Highland Park business SSAs, Aurora, Oak Park, Evanston, Yorkville, etc.). No central public statewide registry or inventory of all SSAs/BIDs located (IDOR has historically tracked 600+ for tax extension/property tax purposes as of ~2011, generating tens of millions annually, but no public list/GIS). Distinguish from unrelated 'Business Districts' under Illinois Municipal Code 65 ILCS 5/11-74.3 (sales-tax increment redevelopment of blighted areas). Coverage authoritative/complete for Chicago; partial for rest of state via scattered county GIS and municipal pages. Failed searches do not prove absence. Enabling URLs: BID https://www.ilga.gov/Legislation/ILCS/Articles?ActID=4518&ChapterID=14 ; SSA article/sections via https://www.ilga.gov/legislation/ILCS/details?MajorTopic=&Chapter=&ActName=Property%20Tax%20Code.&ActID=596&ChapterID=8&ChapAct=35+ILCS+200%2F&SeqStart=86400000&SeqEnd=89400000 and https://ilga.gov/documents/legislation/ilcs/documents/003502000K27-5.htm .
